location.office_time_required: full-time in-person; physically present where startups form

level_signals: 5+ years across founding, growth, GTM, or general management
Owns a P&L and segment number end to end
Reports directly to VP of Growth
Expected to run segment 'like its own business'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have started a company, or you have operated at the founding-team level of an early startup and lived the incorporation-to-traction journey firsthand

You are deeply, currently embedded in the startup community: accelerators, angels, founder networks, the SF startup social scene. You can name the up-and-coming companies before they are obvious, and the community knows you back
5+ years across some combination of founding, growth, GTM, or general management, with clear evidence you can own an outcome end to end rather than one function
A GM instinct: you think in P&L, you make tradeoffs across product, pricing, and marketing, and you are comfortable owning a number with no one to hand it to
Demonstrable AI fluency: specific tools, workflows, or systems you have built or adopted that materially changed your output
Sharp written and verbal communication. You can write the landing page and command the room at the dinner
You live in or will relocate to San Francisco and will be in-market, in-person

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You went through YC, Techstars, Neo, South Park Commons, or an equivalent program, or you ran one
You have an existing audience or following in the founder community
You have built a wildly popular AI tool, or agent that automated a repeatable workflow
Experience with fintech, banking, or developer-led product distribution
Familiarity with comparison and AEO SEO as a land-grab tactic
You have angel invested or advised early companies

explicitly_excluded: Candidates who need a defined playbook handed to them
Candidates who do not want to attend dinners and demo days

scope.surface: Startup segment end to end — acquisition, onboarding, pricing, incentives, positioning, lifecycle, product strategy for newly incorporated US companies
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — corporate card and spend management platform targeting startups at inception
primary_metrics_inferred: 90%+ of all newly incorporated US startups starting on Ramp
Startup segment pipeline and revenue number
core_responsibilities: Own positioning for startup segment vs. Brex, Mercury, and Every
Build full acquisition motion: community/events, founder/angel networks, accelerator partnerships, SEO, paid, referral, incentive-driven growth
Own product strategy for the segment — define day-zero product experience and carry to Product
Own onboarding — make incorporation-to-funded-card the fastest path in market
Own pricing and incentives — design the offer that makes Ramp default at incorporation
Be the face of Ramp in the startup community — host dinners, speak at demo days
Drive toward 90%+ of newly incorporated US startups starting on Ramp; build and instrument the funnel

green_flags: Extremely clear scope — one segment, one brutal metric (90%+ of newly incorporated US startups)
High autonomy and P&L ownership from day one
Reports directly to VP of Growth — short decision chain
Relocation support to SF explicitly offered
Unlimited AI token usage as a benefit signals genuine AI-native culture
red_flags: Hard onsite requirement in San Francisco — eliminates all remote candidates
Founder or founding-team experience is effectively a hard prerequisite, significantly narrowing the pool
Role expects the candidate to already be a known, trusted figure in the SF startup community — very high bar for community credibility
Equity not mentioned anywhere in the JD — unusual for a GM-level role at a high-growth fintech

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from P&L ownership, reports to VP of Growth, and 5+ years requirement; no explicit level stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and stated goal; not listed as a discrete section)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product context)
compensation.equity_mentioned (inferred as false — equity not mentioned anywhere in JD)
ambiguities: Archetype is ambiguous: the role is titled 'Growth Manager' but operates as a Segment GM with P&L ownership, community motion, and product strategy input — closer to BizOps/Strategy & Ops or a Growth GM hybrid than a standard Growth PM or Growth (non-PM).

manages_people: not stated explicitly; the role is described as running 'the segment like its own business' under VP of Growth, suggesting a senior IC GM role without direct reports, but this is not confirmed.
No equity or total comp information provided anywhere in the JD.
